goal: Sub 1:50 (chase 1:45) · Kiprun Singapore Half Marathon · 27 Sep 2026
health_notes: Post-illness recovery (fever 38°C, body aches, cough 16–20 Jun). Two clean runs done 25 Jun and 29 Jun.
Knee niggle (medial, mild) flared once mid-June — resolved with rest. Watch for recurrence.
VO2 Max dipped post-illness but still top 5% for age/gender — expect full return.
key_context: Gym (Snap Fitness Buangkok) opens August — literally across the road.
Columbia OutDry hiking shoes need 50–80km break-in before Norway.
IPPT target: Silver Nov 2026, Gold Feb 2027.
Daily habits: stairs up on return home, push-ups + sit-ups morning routine (resume once fully recovered).

Norway (8–22 Aug): 9 hike days, ~83km total, ~6,059m elevation gain across Senja and Lofoten. ALL hikes rated Hard (one Moderate-Hard). Full schedule: Aug 9 Husfjellet 8.2km/640m; Aug 10 Segla 4.4km/610m + Hesten 5.3km/545m (two hikes same day); Aug 11 Grytetippen+Keipen 8.9km/1002m; Aug 14 Matmora+Delpen 9.7km/835m; Aug 15 Reinebringen 6km/448m; Aug 17 Munkebu+Munken 13km/800m; Aug 18 Ryten+Kvalvika 7.6km/597m; Aug 19 Haen via Hornet 10.3km/582m. Rest days: Aug 8 (travel/arrival), Aug 12-13, Aug 16, Aug 20-22 (travel). Aug 10 and Aug 11 are consecutive hard days with high elevation. This is serious aerobic work — treat as high-volume low-intensity cross-training, NOT a deload. Expect significant leg fatigue during but strong aerobic adaptation after.
July 2026 mileage goal: 100km running. Plan sessions to hit this target while staying injury-free.
No recurring day constraints currently. Schedule runs on any day of the week based on training load and recovery.

hr_zones.notes: Singapore heat — expect 5–8 bpm higher than cool-weather equivalent
